costs.heading: How Much Does an HMO Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £245–£845 depending on property size, number of storeys, and building complexity.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by number of storeys, number of occupants, property age, existing fire safety measures, and local authority requirements. Portfolios of 5+ properties benefit from bulk discount. HMO licensing applications may require additional documentation.

hmo.faq_price_answer: HMO fire risk assessment costs typically range from £245–£845 depending on property size, number of storeys, occupancy levels, and building complexity. Small HMOs (1-2 storeys) start from £245. Use our instant online calculator for an accurate quote tailored to your specific premises.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Fire Risk Assessment for Flats Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £245–£1,200 depending on building size, number of flats, and assessment type required.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by number of flats, number of storeys, building age, existing fire safety measures, and the type of assessment required (Type 1-4). Type 4 assessments involving destructive inspection are significantly more expensive. Multi-block portfolios benefit from bulk discount.

flats.faq_price_answer: Fire risk assessment costs for blocks of flats typically range from £245–£1,200 depending on building size, number of flats, and assessment type required. Type 1 assessments (common areas only) start from £175. Type 4 destructive inspections cost from £980. Use our calculator for an instant estimate.

costs.heading: How Much Does an Office Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £325–£1,135 depending on office size, complexity, and number of employees.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by floor area, number of employees, server room complexity, presence of lithium-ion battery charging, and whether hot-desking arrangements require additional evaluation. Multi-site portfolios benefit from 10-15% bulk discount.

offices.faq_price_answer: Office fire risk assessment costs typically range from £325–£1,135 depending on office size, number of floors, and complexity. Small offices (up to 150m²) start from £325. Large multi-floor offices cost from £925. Use our instant calculator for an accurate quote.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Retail Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £325–£1,120 depending on shop size, number of floors, and complexity of operations.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by floor area, number of floors, presence of commercial kitchen, customer occupancy levels, seasonal display requirements, and whether multi-site standardisation is needed. Multi-site portfolios benefit from 10-15% bulk discount.

retail.faq_price_answer: Retail fire risk assessment costs typically range from £325–£1,120 depending on shop size, number of floors, and type of retail operation. Small shops start from £325. Large stores with multiple floors cost from £910. Use our calculator for an instant estimate.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Warehouse Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £325–£1,130 depending on warehouse size, storage type, and complexity of fire protection systems.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by floor area, storage height, sprinkler system complexity, presence of chemical storage, lithium-ion battery handling, and whether DSEAR assessment is required. Multi-site portfolios benefit from 10-15% bulk discount. Insurance premium reductions of 10-60% are achievable with compliant facilities.

warehouses.faq_price_answer: Warehouse fire risk assessment costs typically range from £325–£1,130 depending on warehouse size, storage type, and complexity of fire protection systems. Small warehouses start from £325. Chemical storage and cold storage facilities may cost more. Use our calculator for an accurate quote.

costs.heading: How Much Does a School Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £675–£2,375 depending on school size, number of buildings, and complexity of facilities.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by number of pupils, number of buildings, science laboratory facilities, assembly hall complexity, after-school club provision, and whether multi-academy trust standardisation is needed. Trusts benefit from 10-15% bulk discount.

schools.faq_price_answer: School fire risk assessment costs typically range from £675–£2,375 depending on school size, number of buildings, and complexity of facilities. Small schools and nurseries start from £675. Multi-academy trusts benefit from discounted per-school rates from £635.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Hospital Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs vary depending on facility size, complexity, and number of departments. Contact us for a detailed quote.

rows.description: Secure or specialist mental health facility

costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by facility size, number of departments, specialist equipment (medical gases, operating theatres), patient mobility considerations, and whether PEEPs for vulnerable patients are required. NHS trust portfolios benefit from significant bulk discount.

hospitals.faq_price_answer: Hospital and healthcare fire risk assessment costs vary significantly depending on facility size, complexity, and number of departments. Please contact us for a detailed quote tailored to your facility.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Hotel Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £375–£1,405 depending on hotel size, number of rooms, and complexity of operations.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by number of rooms, number of floors, presence of commercial kitchen, conference facilities, spa/leisure facilities, and whether disabled guest PEEPs are required. Multi-site portfolios benefit from 10-15% bulk discount.

hotels.faq_price_answer: Hotel fire risk assessment costs typically range from £375–£1,405 depending on hotel size, number of rooms, and complexity of operations. Small hotels and B&Bs start from £375. Large hotels with conference facilities cost from £655. Contact us for a detailed quote.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Care Home Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £275–£825 depending on home size, number of residents, and complexity of care needs.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by number of residents, mobility of residents, presence of specialist care units, building layout complexity, and whether CQC compliance documentation is required. Multi-home operators benefit from bulk discount.

care-homes.faq_price_answer: Care home fire risk assessment costs typically range from £275–£825 depending on home size, number of residents, and complexity of care needs. Small care homes (up to 10 residents) start from £275. Contact us for a detailed quote.

costs.heading: How Much Does a High-Rise Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £625–£3,335+ depending on building height, number of flats, and complexity of fire safety systems.
costs.factors_note: Costs are significantly influenced by building height, number of flats, external wall system type, presence of sprinklers, fire door condition, and whether Building Safety Act 2022 requirements apply. Buildings with non-compliant cladding require specialist assessment. Quarterly fire door checks required for buildings over 11 metres.

high-rise-buildings.faq_price_answer: High-rise building fire risk assessment costs typically range from £625–£3,335+ depending on building height, number of flats, and complexity of fire safety systems. Buildings under 11 metres start from £625. Buildings over 18 metres with cladding issues may cost £2,500+. Contact us for a detailed quote.

costs.heading: How Much Does a Restaurant Fire Risk Assessment Cost?
costs.description: Costs typically range from £325–£1,735 depending on kitchen size, seating capacity, and building complexity.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by kitchen size, number of covers, presence of deep fat fryers, gas installation complexity, extraction system condition, and whether licensing-compliant documentation is required. Multi-site chains benefit from 10-15% bulk discount.

restaurants.faq_price_answer: Restaurant fire risk assessment costs typically range from £325–£1,735 depending on kitchen size, seating capacity, and building complexity. Small restaurants and cafés start from £325. Premises with commercial kitchens and extraction systems cost from £650. Use our calculator for an instant estimate.

costs.heading: Fire Risk Assessment Costs
costs.description: Transparent pricing for fire risk assessments. Costs vary based on building size, complexity, and risk level.
costs.factors_note: Fire risk assessment costs are influenced by: building size (larger premises take longer), complexity (multiple floors, unusual layouts), risk level (high-risk activities, hazardous materials), type of assessment (Type 1-4 for residential), and location. Our cost calculator gives you an instant estimate.

fire-risk-assessment.faq_price_answer: Costs typically range from £245-£500 for small premises, £545-£1,130 for medium commercial buildings, and £625-£1,500+ for large or complex buildings. Hospital assessments are priced on request. Get in touch for an instant estimate.

costs.heading: Fire Alarm Testing Costs
costs.description: Pricing based on the number of devices and the size of the building. The more devices, the lower the unit cost.
costs.factors_note: Several factors affect the price: the number of devices on the system, building access requirements, system age and condition, and whether any parts need replacing. Annual maintenance contracts typically save 15-20% compared to individual bookings.

costs.heading: Fire Extinguisher Servicing Costs
costs.description: Pricing based on the number of extinguishers and whether any units are due for extended service.
costs.factors_note: Extended services cost more due to the discharge, recharge, and parts involved. Water, foam, powder, and wet chemical extinguishers need extended service every 5 years. CO2 extinguishers every 10 years. Annual maintenance contracts typically save 15-20%.

costs.heading: Emergency Light Testing Costs
costs.description: Pricing based on the number of emergency lighting fittings and the size of the building.
costs.factors_note: Costs are influenced by the number of fittings, building access (especially high-level fittings), whether any fittings need replacement, and if central battery systems are present. Annual maintenance contracts typically save 15-20%.

costs.heading: Fire Door Inspection Costs
costs.description: Pricing is based on the number of doors and the size of the building. The more doors, the lower the unit cost.
costs.factors_note: Final cost depends on building access, door locations (e.g. risers, plant rooms), and whether you need a combined survey covering both fire doors and passive fire protection.

costs.heading: Fire Door Survey Costs
costs.description: Pricing is based on the number of doors and the complexity of the building.
costs.factors_note: Final cost depends on building access, door locations (e.g. risers, plant rooms), and whether you need a combined survey covering both fire doors and passive fire protection.

costs.heading: Fire Strategy Costs
costs.description: Costs vary based on project size and complexity.
costs.factors_note: Costs vary based on project size and complexity. We provide detailed quotes after initial consultation and project assessment.

fire-strategy.faq_price_answer: Costs vary based on project size and complexity. Typical ranges are £2,500-£5,000 for small projects, £5,000-£15,000 for medium projects, and £15,000+ for large or complex projects. We provide detailed quotes after initial consultation and project assessment.
